hero_subtitle: SFTY streamlines fragmented provider workflows into a unified compliance operating layer that reduces risk, enhances audit readiness and provides clear oversight for digital asset businesses.

problem_title: Operational risk grows with provider dependency
problem_description: Executives face increasing operational gaps when compliance providers operate in isolation. This fragmentation slows decision-making, weakens audit readiness and limits clear oversight across critical workflows.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY connects multiple compliance providers into one scalable control layer that centralizes workflows, preserves audit evidence, and gives executives a unified view to manage operational risk effectively.
workflow_title: From provider outputs to operational risk control
workflow_description: SFTY consolidates multiple provider results into a unified workflow that supports risk review, decision logging, evidence preservation and executive compliance visibility.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_title: Not another provider. The control layer

why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view

authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review
authority_card_4_description: Support private environments, permissions, integrations and internal governance

ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for executive risk control?
faq_1_answer: SFTY offers executives a connected compliance control layer that improves risk visibility, reduces manual workload, and strengthens audit readiness across multi-provider digital asset workflows.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY reduce provider dependency risk?
faq_2_answer: SFTY connects outputs from multiple compliance providers into one structured operational workflow, reducing reliance on any single provider and mitigating fragmentation risks across compliance processes.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve audit-ready evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves decision logs, case evidence, regulatory change records and audit trails in secured records to support defensible reporting and inspection readiness.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing compliance stacks?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY integrates with existing KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers to connect fragmented data into a centralized compliance operating system without disrupting current tools.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace existing compliance providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY does not replace providers but sits above them, structuring their outputs into workflows, cases, evidence and reports for comprehensive compliance management.
final_title: Reduce operational risk with connected compliance workflows
final_description: Review how SFTY connects fragmented provider outputs into a single operational view for clearer executive oversight and audit readiness.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
